'bitterly'라는 단어를 선택하여 의료계의 반발이 논리적 근거보다는 감정적 편견에 기반하고 있음을 암시하며, 부정적인 영향력은 인정하면서 긍정적인 영향력은 부정하는 전문가들의 이중성을 효과적으로 대조시켰습니다.", "output": "I have always wondered why so many people in the medical profession write about iatrogenic disease, and then speak and write so bitterly against the use of suggestion for health and well-being."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_UTILIZATION", "context_analysis": {"topic": "The necessity of patient-centered language in pain management", "tone_constraint": "Authoritative, instructional, and emphasizing clinical precision", "structural_constraint": "Direct professional advice focusing on the patient's subjective lexicon"}, "instruction": "Explain to a trainee therapist why they must let a patient describe their pain in their own specific language and in great detail, using an authoritative yet subtle tone.", "reasoning_trace": "1. Identify the core principle: Utilization. To change a patient's reality, the therapist must first accept and use the patient's own internal map. 2. Recognize that medical or standardized terminology often fails to resonate with the patient's unconscious mind. 3. Formulate a rationale: The patient's specific adjectives are the 'keys' to their sensory experience. By gathering these exact words, the therapist acquires the specific linguistic tools needed for later hypnotic intervention or reframing. 4. Emphasize the 'fullness' of the description: This serves two purposes—it ensures no detail is missed for utilization, and the act of detailed description itself can begin the process of sensory dissociation or focus-shifting. 5. Use the intensifier 'awfully important' to signal the gravity of this step in the therapeutic process, ensuring the trainee understands this is not a mere formality but a foundational requirement.", "output": "It is awfully important to have your patient describe his pain in his own words, and to have him describe it fully."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_UTILIZATION_REDEFINITION", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Pain management and hypnotic induction for terminal cancer", "tone_constraint": "Clinical, instructional, and subtly hypnotic", "structural_constraint": "Case narrative transitioning from symptom analysis to physiological redefinition"}, "instruction": "Describe your clinical strategy for treating an 83-year-old physician's carcinomatosis pain, explaining why you prioritized certain sensations to facilitate hypnotic relaxation.", "reasoning_trace": "First, I must categorize the patient's subjective experiences of pain into sensory clusters. The sharp, lancinating pains represent high-intensity neurological firing, while the heavy, dragging pains represent a physiological state closer to lethargy. My strategic goal is to induce relaxation, which is fundamentally a state of inertia. By selecting the 'heavy' and 'dragging' sensations already present in the patient's awareness, I utilize his existing symptoms as a foundation for the hypnotic state. I redefine 'heaviness'—previously a source of suffering—as the primary component of relaxation. This 'utilization' technique minimizes resistance because I am not asking the patient to feel something new, but rather to re-interpret what he is already feeling. I then use repetitive, rhythmic language to reinforce the sensation of inertia, leading the patient from a state of pain to a state of hypnotic immobility where the limbs become 'too heavy to lift'.", "output": "I can think of the 83-year-old physician whom I treated for the pain of his carcinomatosis. He described his pain as sharp, as lancinating, as stabbing, as cutting, as burning. He also described a heavy, dragging pain—a heavy, dragging, throbbing pain. Now which pain would I approach first? I chose the heavy, throbbing, dragging pain, because the first thing I wanted to do was to teach that man what relaxation is in hypnosis. And what is relaxation? Relaxation is a feeling of inertia—an unwillingness to move. Your legs are too heavy to lift, your arms are"}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_UTILIZATION_RHYTHM", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Utilizing physiological rhythm for relaxation", "tone_constraint": "Rhythmic, soothing, and repetitive", "structural_constraint": "Short, repetitive phrases synchronized with a physiological pulse"}, "instruction": "Explain and demonstrate how to use a patient's rhythmic throbbing pain as a tool for relaxation in the style of Milton Erickson.", "reasoning_trace": "To achieve the goal of inducing relaxation through a symptom, I first apply the principle of 'Utilization.' Instead of suggesting the patient ignore the throbbing pain, I acknowledge its presence and, more importantly, its rhythm. I observe the frequency of the pulse or throb and time my verbal suggestions to match that exact cadence. By doing so, the pain itself becomes the metronome for the hypnotic induction. I use the word 'relaxed' repeatedly, synchronizing each utterance with the patient's internal rhythm. This creates a powerful pacing effect where the patient's own body dictates the speed of the suggestion, making it nearly impossible for the conscious mind to reject the transition from discomfort to relaxation.", "output": "Your legs are getting more relaxed . relaxed ... relaxed"}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_STRATEGIC_AMNESIA", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Avoiding the reinforcement of symptoms through direct verbal reminders", "tone_constraint": "Pragmatic, protective, and focused on future-oriented change", "structural_constraint": "A declarative statement of clinical boundaries followed by a psychological justification for indirectness"}, "instruction": "Explain your clinical strategy regarding how you handle a patient's sensitive problem, like bedwetting, after they have initially disclosed it to you.", "reasoning_trace": "To facilitate change in a patient with a sensitive issue like enuresis, I must avoid the 're-traumatization' of the conscious mind. Constant reminders of the 'wet bed' only serve to anchor the patient to their failure and shame, strengthening the neural pathways associated with the problem. My reasoning is that once the information is shared, it is 'in the room' and doesn't need to be highlighted. By strategically 'forgetting' or refusing to mention the problem directly, I bypass the patient's conscious defenses and shame. This creates a psychological space where the unconscious can focus on the new goal—dryness and physical control—without the interference of conscious embarrassment. I move from the 'problem state' to the 'resource state' by simply refusing to dwell on the symptom.", "output": "Once a patient has told me about the 'wet bed', I see no reason to ever mention it again. They are already far too aware of it, and reminding them only reinforces the very thing they want to change. My interest is not in the dampness of the past, but in the dry, comfortable awakening of their future. By not speaking of it, I allow their mind to move away from the problem and toward the natural capacity for control that they haven't yet realized they possess."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_ANALOGY_SENSORY_HABITUATION", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Pain transformation through sensory habituation", "tone_constraint": "Gentle, narrative, suggestive, and non-confrontational", "structural_constraint": "Starts with a general possibility, moves to a relatable sensory experience, and ends with a subtle metaphor for the fading of sensation"}, "instruction": "내담자가 고통의 감각을 변화시키고 완화할 수 있도록, 감각의 적응과 변화를 설명하는 에릭슨 스타일의 은유적 대화를 구성하십시오.", "reasoning_trace": "1. Establish Possibility: Begin with a broad statement ('certain transformations') to set the stage for change without specifying how yet. 2. Utilize Truisms: Use the 'You know how...' structure to elicit an internal 'Yes' response, building rapport and agreement. 3. Sensory Analogy: Select a universal sensory experience (eating dessert) that involves a positive-to-neutral shift. This makes the idea of a sensation fading feel natural and inevitable. 4. Temporal Distortion/Repetition: Mention the 'sixty-sixth mouthful.' This specific, high number emphasizes the process of habituation and the passage of time/experience leading to change. 5. Reframing the Sensation: Describe the change not as something becoming 'bad,' but as 'dying out.' This provides a linguistic bridge for the patient to perceive their pain not as an active enemy, but as a sensation that can simply lose its intensity and fade away. 6. Indirect Suggestion: By talking about dessert, the conscious mind stays relaxed while the unconscious mind applies the principle of 'dying out' to the actual problem (pain).", "output": "There are certain transformations that can be brought about here. You know how that first mouthful of dessert tastes so very good? And even the second mouthful still tastes good; but by the time you reach the sixty-sixth mouthful, it doesn’t taste so good. You have lost the liking for it, and the taste of the dessert has changed in some peculiar way. It hasn’t become bad; it has just “died out” in flavor."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_ILLUSION_OF_CHOICE", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Prioritizing physical sensations for therapeutic intervention", "tone_constraint": "Gentle, authoritative yet permissive, and focused on the patient's internal experience", "structural_constraint": "A pacing statement followed by a directive that offers a choice within a presupposed framework"}, "instruction": "내담자가 자신의 신체 감각 중 하나를 선택하여 치료를 시작하도록 유도하되, 선택의 자율성을 부여하면서도 치료가 진행될 것임을 전제로 하는 에릭슨적 대화 기법을 사용하세요.", "reasoning_trace": "먼저 내담자가 이미 인지하고 있는 신체적 감각들을 언급함으로써 현재의 상태를 '페이싱(Pacing)'합니다. '당신이 나에게 설명했던'이라는 표현을 통해 내담자의 기여를 인정하고 라포를 강화합니다. 핵심 기법으로 '선택의 환상(Illusion of Choice)'을 사용합니다. '치료를 할 것인가 말 것인가'가 아니라 '어떤 것을 먼저 할 것인가'를 묻는 '먼저(first)'라는 단어를 배치함으로써, 모든 감각이 결국 치료될 것이라는 전제(Presupposition)를 무의식에 심습니다. 이를 통해 내담자는 저항 없이 치료 과정에 참여하게 됩니다.", "output": "Now, as you pay attention to these various sensations in your body that you have described to me, I would like you to name the particular sensation that you want me to work on first."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_METAPHOR_UTILIZATION", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Pain management through amnesia and focused attention", "tone_constraint": "Conversational, permissive, and naturalistic", "structural_constraint": "Analogy-based framing followed by a post-hypnotic temporal suggestion"}, "instruction": "Explain the concept of therapeutic amnesia for pain control by utilizing a common, relatable experience such as being absorbed in a movie, and include a suggestion for delayed recall to reinforce the effect.", "reasoning_trace": "To bypass the patient's conscious resistance to 'just forgetting' pain, I must frame amnesia as a natural, everyday phenomenon rather than a difficult task. First, I introduce the idea of 'forgetting' as a valid coping mechanism. Second, I use the 'movie' metaphor because it is a universal experience of a naturally occurring trance state where external stimuli (the screen) override internal sensations (the headache). Third, I use the word 'absorbed' to describe the hypnotic focus. Finally, I add a temporal distortion element—suggesting that the realization of having had pain might only occur days later—which effectively plants a post-hypnotic suggestion that the pain is already a thing of the past and irrelevant to the present moment.", "output": "One of the ways of dealing with unpleasant sensations is to forget them—like when you go to the movies and get all absorbed in the suspenseful drama on screen, and meanwhile you forget about your headache. You may not remember until three days later that you had had a headache when you went into the cinema."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_UTILIZATION_MODIFICATION", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Pain management through sensory modification", "tone_constraint": "Calm, observant, and subtly authoritative", "structural_constraint": "Conditional 'If-Then' structure combined with sensory pacing and leading"}, "instruction": "내담자가 호소하는 고통스러운 감각을 그대로 수용(Pacing)하면서, 그 감각의 특성을 미묘하게 변화(Leading)시켜 통증 조절의 가능성을 열어주는 에릭슨 스타일의 암시를 작성하세요.", "reasoning_trace": "먼저 내담자가 사용한 'grinding pain(갈리는 듯한 통증)'이라는 표현을 그대로 활용하여 내담자의 현재 주관적 현실에 동조(Pacing)합니다. 'If you will just pay attention(그저 주의를 기울여 본다면)'이라는 표현을 통해 내담자의 초점을 내부의 감각으로 유도하며 최면적 몰입을 강화합니다. 그 다음, 내담자의 원래 묘사에 'slow(느린)'라는 형용사를 덧붙임으로써 통증의 리듬을 재정의합니다. 이는 통증의 성질이 고정된 것이 아니라 변화할 수 있음을 무의식에 암시하며, 통증의 강도를 완화하거나 통제할 수 있는 심리적 여지를 마련하는 전략적 개입입니다.", "output": "If you will just pay attention to that grinding pain you will notice that it is a slow grinding pain."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_AMNESIA_REFRAMING", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Chronic pain management through temporal isolation and induced amnesia", "tone_constraint": "Logical, authoritative, yet permissive and peer-oriented for a fellow physician", "structural_constraint": "Direct suggestion combined with rhetorical questioning to devalue past memories"}, "instruction": "82세 의사인 내담자가 겪는 극심한 통증을 관리하기 위해, 과거의 통증 기억을 지우고 오직 현재의 통증에만 집중하도록 유도하는 최면적 암시를 작성하라.", "reasoning_trace": "먼저, 내담자가 고령의 의사라는 점을 고려하여 논리적이고 합리적인 설득 방식을 취한다. 만성 통증의 고통은 과거의 기억이 현재의 감각에 중첩될 때 극대화된다는 점에 착안하여, '망각(Amnesia)'을 치료적 도구로 활용한다. '어제의 통증을 기억할 필요가 없다'는 허용적 암시를 통해 심리적 부담을 덜어주고, 과거의 고통을 회상하는 행위를 '무의미한 일(nonsensical)'로 규정하여 내담자의 비판적 사고가 망각을 수용하도록 유도한다. 시간적 범위를 어제, 1주일 전, 2주일 전으로 확장하며 과거의 고통을 현재로부터 멀어지게 밀어낸 뒤, 의식을 오직 '지금 이 순간'의 감각으로만 좁히게 함으로써 통증의 총체적 무게를 분산시킨다.", "output": "you really don’t need to remember yesterday’s attacks of lancinating pain. It is enough to experience the attacks of lancinating pain you are feeling at the present moment. That is enough. Why bother to remember yesterday’s pains, and the pains of a week ago, and the pains of two weeks ago! It seems nonsensical to me. Give your attention to the pain you are having right now."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_TEMPORAL_DISSOCIATION", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Managing the anticipation of chronic recurring pain in an elderly physician", "tone_constraint": "Gentle, permissive, yet psychologically directive and authoritative", "structural_constraint": "Starts with a rhetorical question, shifts focus to the present, and concludes with a suggestion for amnesia"}, "instruction": "Help a patient suffering from recurring pain attacks to stop anticipating future suffering by utilizing their current focus and suggesting the possibility of forgetting past and future pain.", "reasoning_trace": "To alleviate the patient's anxiety about future pain, I first use a rhetorical question ('And do you really need to remember...?') to challenge the utility of their anticipation. I intentionally use vivid, clinical descriptors like 'sharp, shooting, lancinating' to mirror the patient's medical background while simultaneously making the thought of remembering such pain unappealing. I then utilize the 'present moment' as a container for their attention, suggesting they 'keep very, very busy' with only today's experience, thereby narrowing their temporal field. Finally, I employ permissive suggestions for amnesia ('You don't have to think,' 'You don't even need to remember') to lower conscious resistance, and frame the end of a pain attack as a transition into a 'forgotten past,' effectively severing the psychological link between the experience and the memory of it.", "output": "And do you really need to remember that tomorrow, every 20 minutes, you are going to have those sharp, shooting, lancinating, cutting pains? I think you can keep very, very busy with just the pain you are experiencing today. You don’t have to think about tomorrow. You don’t even need to remember that tomorrow you will have further attacks. And once you have had an attack of pain, I think it would be awfully nice just to forget about it, to put it in the forgotten past."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_DIRECT_UTILIZATION", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Displaced pain and clinical directness with a peer", "tone_constraint": "Brutally honest, authoritative, and medically direct", "structural_constraint": "A concise, impactful nominal phrase focusing on the existential threat of the symptom"}, "instruction": "Address an elderly physician regarding his displaced pain using the most direct and unfiltered language possible to bypass his professional ego.", "reasoning_trace": "To reach a highly intellectualized patient like an 82-year-old physician, Erickson bypasses subtle metaphors and employs 'clinical shock.' By using the phrase 'the pain that is killing you,' he utilizes the patient's own medical understanding of mortality. The word 'killing' serves as a powerful hypnotic suggestion that demands immediate attention from the unconscious mind, stripping away the defense mechanism of displacement. It reframes the symptom not as a chronic nuisance, but as a lethal force, thereby motivating the patient to confront the underlying psychological cause. This directness is a form of rapport-building with a peer who values blunt diagnostic truth over therapeutic pleasantries.", "output": "the pain that is killing you"}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_PAIN_DISPLACEMENT", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Pain management for a child through sensory redirection", "tone_constraint": "Gentle, authoritative, and matter-of-fact", "structural_constraint": "Simple vocabulary for a child, repetitive spatial markers, and soft suggestive phrasing"}, "instruction": "A child is experiencing localized pain. Use a conversational hypnosis technique to help them manage it by suggesting the pain move or spread to other areas to dilute its intensity.", "reasoning_trace": "1. Pacing the Reality: Start by acknowledging the child's current experience ('It hurts right there') to establish rapport and validate their feeling. 2. Introducing Suggestion: Use the phrase 'I think it should' to introduce a new possibility without being overly demanding, leveraging the therapist's authority in a soft way. 3. Displacement and Diffusion: Instead of trying to remove the pain (which the child might resist), suggest spreading it to other locations. This dilutes the concentration of the sensation. 4. Spatial Anchoring: Use 'here' and 'there' to guide the child's internal focus and attention away from the original site of pain. 5. Cognitive Reframing: By making the pain move, the child learns that the sensation is fluid and controllable, which is the first step toward anesthesia or dissociation.", "output": "It hurts right there, but I think it should hurt here, too, and a little bit here, and some right there."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_PACING_LEADING_SENSORY_SHIFT", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Pain management for a child through sensory redirection", "tone_constraint": "Gentle, reassuring, and authoritative yet permissive", "structural_constraint": "Simple language suitable for a child, using present continuous tense to imply ongoing change"}, "instruction": "A child is experiencing physical discomfort. Use conversational hypnosis to redirect their attention toward positive sensations and suggest the gradual onset of relief without creating resistance.", "reasoning_trace": "First, I use the word 'Now' to anchor the child's attention to the present moment and signal a transition. Second, I employ the phrase 'beginning to feel better' rather than claiming the pain is gone; this is a 'pacing' technique that acknowledges the process of change, making the suggestion more believable and harder for the conscious mind to reject. Third, I use the vague predicate 'nice sensations' to allow the child's unconscious mind to fill in the specific details—whether it be warmth, coolness, or numbness—that are most comfortable for them. By shifting the focus from the location of pain to the emergence of positive feelings, I facilitate a sensory reorientation that naturally diminishes the perception of discomfort.", "output": "Now the pain there is beginning to feel better. You are getting nice sensations."}

{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_DISSOCIATION_SPATIAL", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Facilitating a dissociative experience through spatial displacement", "tone_constraint": "Calm, directive, and sensory-focused", "structural_constraint": "Sequential instructions moving from physical action to internal sensory projection"}, "instruction": "Jane에게 해리(dissociation) 경험을 유도하기 위해, 그녀가 방 건너편에 앉아 자신을 바라보고 있는 것처럼 느끼게 하는 최면적 지시를 내리세요.", "reasoning_trace": "먼저 '눈을 뜨라'는 단순한 신체적 명령으로 의식을 깨우는 동시에 최면적 트랜스 상태를 유지하게 합니다. 그 다음, 즉시 의식의 위치를 현재의 물리적 신체 밖인 '저기 건너편(over there)'으로 이동시키도록 유도합니다. {"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_PACING_AND_LEADING", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Spatial perception and focus guidance", "tone_constraint": "Observational, calm, and authoritative", "structural_constraint": "Cause-effect linkage using truisms (Since A and B, then C)"}, "instruction": "내담자의 현재 신체적 감각과 주의 집중 상태를 활용하여, 치료자의 다음 행동에 몰입하게 만드는 최면적 유도 문장을 작성하십시오.", "reasoning_trace": "먼저 내담자가 부정할 수 없는 현재의 물리적 사실인 '편안하게 앉아 있음'과 '관찰하고 있음'을 언급하여 '예스 세트(Yes-set)'를 형성합니다. 'Since'라는 접속사를 사용하여 이러한 자명한 이치(Truisms)를 전제로 깔고, 이를 자연스럽게 치료자의 다음 행동에 주목하라는 지시와 논리적으로 연결합니다. 이는 내담자의 비판적 사고를 우회하며 무의식적인 집중과 기대감을 이끌어내는 전형적인 에릭슨의 '페이스-페이스-리드(Pace-Pace-Lead)' 기법을 적용한 것입니다.", "output": "Since you feel yourself sitting comfortable over there, and you are watching me so carefully, notice what I am going to do next."}

Utilize their resistance as a character trait, point out a physiological fact they just witnessed, and offer a direct verification to bypass their skepticism.", "reasoning_trace": "First, I must address the mother's skepticism directly but not defensively. By stating she likes to 'learn the hard way,' I am utilizing her resistance and framing it as a learning style, which creates a psychological bind—she cannot disagree without proving she is even more difficult. Second, I draw her attention to a sensory fact: the daughter's lack of a wince. This is a truism based on immediate observation that her conscious mind cannot easily refute. This forces the mother to confront the subjective reality of the patient rather than her own preconceived notions, effectively using a 'double bind' where the evidence is undeniable regardless of her skepticism.", "output": "Mother, you like to learn things the hard way, don’t you. A girl that looks like you isn’t always going to remain a girl."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_CONFUSION_DISORIENTATION", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Pain management for children through cognitive disorientation", "tone_constraint": "Gentle, inquisitive, and seemingly naive curiosity", "structural_constraint": "Sequential questioning that shifts from internal sensation to external categorization"}, "instruction": "신체적 통증을 겪고 있는 아이에게 주의를 분산시키고, 통증의 위치에 대한 인지적 혼란을 야기하여 고통을 완화하는 밀턴 에릭슨의 대화 기법을 사용하여 말해보세요.", "reasoning_trace": "먼저 아이의 주의를 내부의 통증 감각에서 외부의 신체 부위 명칭으로 전환시킵니다. '오른쪽인가, 왼쪽인가'라는 이분법적 질문을 통해 아이가 자신의 신체를 객관적으로 바라보게 유도합니다(Dissociation). 그 다음 단계로, 너무나 당연한 사실인 '어느 쪽이 오른쪽이고 왼쪽인지'를 다시 묻음으로써 아이의 의식적인 논리 체계에 가벼운 혼란(Confusion)을 일으킵니다. 아이가 어느 다리가 어느 쪽인지 판단하려 애쓰는 동안, 뇌의 인지 자원은 통증 신호 처리보다 공간 식별 작업에 우선순위를 두게 되며, 결과적으로 통증에 대한 자각이 약화되거나 소거되는 효과를 노린 것입니다.", "output": "어디 보자, 그 통증이 네 오른쪽 다리에 있니, 아니면 왼쪽 다리에 있니? ... 가만있어 보자, 어느 쪽이 네 왼쪽 다리고 어느 쪽이 네 오른쪽 다리였더라?"}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_CONFUSION_ILLUSION_OF_CHOICE", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Pain management through sensory localization and confusion", "tone_constraint": "Gentle, inquisitive, and cognitively demanding", "structural_constraint": "Binary choice question with redundant linguistic markers"}, "instruction": "아이의 통증 감각을 완화하기 위해, 통증의 위치를 구체적으로 정의하도록 유도하면서도 'outside side', 'inside side'와 같은 중첩된 표현을 사용하여 의식적인 혼란을 야기하고 주의를 분산시키십시오.", "reasoning_trace": "첫째, 아이의 주의를 통증의 '고통'이라는 주관적 느낌에서 '위치'라는 객관적/공간적 분석으로 전환시킨다. 둘째, 'outside side'와 'inside side'라는 다소 부자연스럽고 반복적인 어휘를 사용하여 아이의 논리적 사고 체계에 가벼운 과부하(Confusion)를 준다. 셋째, 'A인가 아니면 B인가?'라는 이분법적 질문(Double Bind의 변형)을 통해 아이가 반드시 둘 중 하나를 선택하게 함으로써, 통증이 그 외의 영역에는 존재하지 않거나 통제 가능하다는 암시를 무의식에 심는다. 넷째, 이러한 복잡한 위치 찾기 과정은 뇌의 전두엽을 활성화시켜 통증을 처리하는 변연계의 활동을 상대적으로 억제하는 효과를 노린다.", "output": "And now is it on the outside side of your leg, or is it on the inside side of your leg?"}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_FRACTIONATION_REDUCTION", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Pain management through incremental suggestion and mathematical truisms", "tone_constraint": "Calm, humble, logical, and repetitive to induce a trance-like acceptance of change.", "structural_constraint": "Begins with a disclaimer of limitation to bypass resistance, followed by a series of repetitive, logically irrefutable cycles of small percentage reductions."}, "instruction": "내담자가 느끼는 압도적인 고통을 한 번에 해결하려 하지 말고, 작은 백분율 단위로 나누어 점진적으로 줄여나가는 에릭슨의 대화형 최면 기법을 사용하여 말해보세요. 특히 '모든 것을 할 수는 없다'는 겸손한 태도로 저항을 낮추고, 논리적으로 반박할 수 없는 수치를 활용하여 무의식이 변화를 받아들이게 하세요.", "reasoning_trace": "첫째, '모든 고통을 없앨 수 없다'는 부정적 암시(Negative Suggestion)를 통해 내담자의 의식적 저항을 무력화합니다. 이는 치료자가 전지전능하지 않음을 시인함으로써 내담자의 신뢰를 얻는 고도의 전략입니다. 둘째, 고통을 1%, 5%, 10%와 같은 작은 조각으로 분절(Fractionation)합니다. 1%의 소실은 의식적으로는 '눈에 띄지 않는' 사소한 일이지만, 무의식적으로는 '고통이 줄어들고 있다'는 사실을 수용하게 만듭니다. 셋째, 수학적 진실(Truism)을 사용합니다. 1%를 잃어도 99%가 남는다는 논리는 의식이 반박할 수 없는 사실이며, 이 과정에서 '고통의 상실(loss of pain)'이라는 긍정적 변화를 자연스럽게 전제(Presupposition)로 깔아둡니다. 넷째, 반복적인 문장 구조를 통해 내담자를 가벼운 트랜스 상태로 유도하며 점진적으로 더 큰 변화(10% 이상)를 수용할 준비를 시킵니다.", "output": "I can’t take away all of your pain. That is asking too much of me; it is asking too much of your body. And if you lose 1 percent of that pain you would still have 99 percent of it left; you wouldn’t notice the loss of 1 percent, but it would still be a loss of 1 percent. You could lose 5 percent of that pain. You wouldn’t notice the loss of 5 percent, because you would still have 95 percent of the pain; but you would still have a loss of 5 percent. Now you could lose 10 percent of the pain, but that really wouldn’t be noticeable because you would still have 90 percent of it; but you nevertheless would have a loss of 10 percent of your pain..."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_NEGOTIATION_PRESUPPOSITION", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Negotiating the degree of pain reduction with a patient", "tone_constraint": "Authoritative yet seemingly cautious and collaborative", "structural_constraint": "High-anchor suggestion followed by a strategic reduction to a slightly lower but significant target"}, "instruction": "내담자가 통증 완화의 가능성을 현실적으로 받아들이면서도 무의식적으로는 매우 높은 수준의 치유를 수용하도록, 높은 수치를 먼저 제안했다가 약간 하향 조정하는 에릭슨의 협상 기법을 사용하여 발화하십시오.", "reasoning_trace": "먼저 '80%'라는 높은 수치를 언급하여 내담자의 무의식에 강력한 치유의 기준점(Anchor)을 설정한다. 그 직후 '아직은 합리적이지 않다'는 부정적 피드백을 주어 내담자의 의식적인 저항이나 회의감을 미리 차단하고 신뢰를 구축한다. '아직(yet)'이라는 단어를 사용하여 향후에는 80%도 가능하다는 암시를 남긴다. 최종적으로 '75%'로 타협하는 척 제안함으로써, 내담자가 75%라는 거대한 통증 완화 수치를 '합리적이고 달성 가능한 목표'로 기꺼이 수용하게 만든다. 이는 통증이 사라질 것이라는 사실 자체를 전제(Presupposition)로 깔고 가는 고도의 전략이다.", "output": "You might even lose 80 percent of your pain, but I don’t think that is quite reasonable, yet. I would be willing to settle for a loss of 75 percent."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_INCREMENTAL_NEGOTIATION", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Negotiation of pain reduction percentages", "tone_constraint": "Calm, persuasive, and authoritative yet permissive", "structural_constraint": "Use of numerical comparison, time-distortion truisms (sooner or later), and anchoring (but first)"}, "instruction": "Act as Milton Erickson. Negotiate with a patient to reduce their pain levels by moving from a known state to a slightly better one, using incremental suggestions and language that implies the inevitability of progress.", "reasoning_trace": "First, I will minimize the perceived gap between the current state and the goal by asking for the 'difference' between 75 and 80 percent, making the change seem trivial. Second, I will use the temporal truism 'sooner or later' to embed the suggestion that losing 80 percent of the pain is an inevitable future event, bypassing conscious resistance. Third, I will introduce an even higher goal (85 percent) to serve as a psychological anchor; this makes the 80 percent target feel more conservative and attainable by comparison. Finally, I will use the phrase 'but first, let us settle' to focus the unconscious mind on the immediate task of accepting the 80 percent reduction, while leaving the door open for further improvement later.", "output": "What is the difference between 75 and 80 percent, and sooner or later you can lose 80 percent, and maybe 85 percent; but first, let us settle for 80."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_COMPLIANCE_SET", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Overcoming resistance through physical movement and compliance building", "tone_constraint": "Polite, matter-of-fact, authoritative yet gentle", "structural_constraint": "A sequence of simple, low-stakes physical commands followed by a psychological state suggestion"}, "instruction": "저항이 심한 내담자를 다룰 때, 신체적인 움직임을 유도하여 무의식적인 순응(Compliance)을 이끌어내고 최종적으로 편안한 상태로 유도하는 밀턴 에릭슨의 대화 기법을 사용해 보세요.", "reasoning_trace": "에릭슨은 내담자의 심리적 저항을 직접 돌파하려 하지 않고, 대신 신체적 차원에서의 작은 협조를 이끌어내는 전략을 취합니다. 첫째, '일어서기', '무게 중심 옮기기'와 같이 거부할 이유가 없는 단순한 신체적 요구를 연속적으로 제시하여 내담자의 잠재의식 속에 '치료자의 지시를 따른다'는 관성을 형성합니다(Compliance Set). 둘째, 'And now', 'Now', 'Then'과 같은 연결어를 사용하여 일련의 행동을 하나의 흐름으로 묶어 비판적 사고가 개입할 틈을 주지 않습니다. 셋째, 손의 위치를 구체적으로 지정하는 반복적인 행위를 통해 내담자의 주의력을 신체 감각에 집중시킵니다. 마지막으로, 이러한 신체적 순응이 최고조에 달했을 때 '매우 편안해질 것'이라는 심리적 암시를 삽입하여, 신체적 이완이 심리적 수용으로 자연스럽게 전이되도록 설계했습니다.", "output": "Do you mind standing up? Will you please place your weight primarily on your right foot? And now shift your weight to your left foot. Now will you please sit down, and put your hand on the arm of the chair there; now put it here; now put it on the other arm; then put it there. Now, I think you will feel very comfortable."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_REFRAMING_ANALOGY", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Reframing the perceived danger of hypnosis through a common analogy", "tone_constraint": "Non-confrontational, matter-of-fact, reassuring, and authoritative", "structural_constraint": "Utilizes a 'Yes, and' approach followed by a metaphorical comparison to shift the focus from 'danger' to 'skillful application'."}, "instruction": "A patient reports that another doctor warned them hypnosis is dangerous. Respond as Milton Erickson by using an everyday analogy to reframe the concept of danger, shifting the focus toward your professional commitment to safety and gentleness without directly criticizing the other doctor.", "reasoning_trace": "First, I will acknowledge the patient's statement with a simple 'Yes, you know,' which avoids creating resistance by not contradicting the previous doctor's warning. Second, I will introduce the analogy of driving an automobile—a high-risk activity that is universally accepted when handled correctly. Third, I will validate the existence of danger ('100 ways of driving dangerously') to align with the patient's current reality, but then immediately pivot to my own lack of interest in those dangers. This subtly suggests that focusing on danger is unnecessary for a skilled practitioner. Fourth, I will define my professional identity as someone who 'prefers just to drive it safely.' Finally, I will bridge the analogy back to hypnosis and use a rhythmic triad of positive qualifiers—'safely, carefully, and gently'—to embed a sense of security and comfort in the patient's unconscious mind.", "output": "Yes, you know, it is something like driving an automobile. There are at least 100 ways of driving an automobile dangerously; maybe more than 100. I have never bothered to try to find out all the dangerous ways of driving an automobile because I prefer just to drive it safely. And that is the way I feel about hypnosis. I like to use it safely, carefully, and gently."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_UTILIZATION_NORMALIZATION", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Addressing resistance to hypnosis", "tone_constraint": "Accepting, permissive, and authoritative yet gentle", "structural_constraint": "Normalization via analogy followed by a time-limited double bind"}, "instruction": "내담자가 최면에 저항감을 보일 때, 그 저항을 자연스러운 학습 과정으로 재정의(Reframing)하고 이중 구속(Double Bind)을 사용하여 최면 유도를 진행하는 에릭슨의 대화 방식을 재현하십시오.", "reasoning_trace": "먼저 'Naturally'라는 단어를 사용하여 내담자의 현재 상태를 즉각적으로 긍정하고 수용합니다. 이는 내담자의 방어 기제를 완화하는 전략적 페이싱(Pacing)입니다. 그 다음, 롤러스케이트나 춤을 배우는 것과 같은 보편적인 과거의 학습 경험을 소환하여, 현재의 저항이 '새로운 기술을 습득하기 전의 당연한 망설임'이라는 트루이즘(Truism)을 구축합니다. 'Why shouldn't you?'라는 질문을 통해 저항을 정당화함으로써 내담자의 심리적 반발을 무력화하고, 저항의 의미를 '거부'가 아닌 '준비 시간의 필요성'으로 재정의(Reframing)합니다. 마지막으로, '지금 바로 들어갈 것인가' 아니면 '1~5초만 더 기다렸다가 들어갈 것인가'라는 이중 구속(Double Bind)을 제시하여, 내담자가 어떤 선택을 하든 결국 최면 상태로 진입(Leading)하게끔 유도합니다.", "output": "Naturally, you are unfamiliar with hypnosis. When you were first asked to learn a difficult task, such as roller skating or ice skating, you were also hesitant. When you undertook dance lessons you were hesitant. You showed a certain amount of resistance. Why shouldn’t you? Your resistance meant that you were not quite certain you were ready to start the dancing, or the roller skating, or whatever. And now the question is, Are you really ready to go into a hypnotic trance, or shall we wait one, two, three, four, or even five seconds longer, until you really get ready?"}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_UTILIZATION_RESISTANCE", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Transforming resistance into hypnotic cooperation", "tone_constraint": "Permissive, paradoxical, and focused", "structural_constraint": "Double bind structure using questions and specific physical suggestions"}, "instruction": "내담자의 저항을 억누르지 않고 오히려 이를 활용하여 최면적 반응을 유도하는 방식으로 말해주세요. 내담자가 저항을 증명하기 위해 특정 신체 부위를 고정하도록 유도하여, 저항하는 행위가 곧 암시에 순응하는 결과가 되도록 설계해야 합니다.", "reasoning_trace": "내담자의 저항을 문제로 보지 않고 최면을 위한 도구로 활용하기 위해 저항의 방식을 직접 선택하게 합니다. 손을 움직이지 않는 행위를 저항의 수단으로 정의함으로써, 내담자가 저항을 지속할수록 역설적으로 신체적 고정 상태라는 최면적 현상에 깊이 빠져들게 만드는 이중 구속을 적용합니다.", "output": "당신은 어떤 방식으로 당신의 저항을 보여주는 것이 가장 좋겠습니까? 지금 당장 이 세상 그 무엇도 당신의 오른손을 허벅지에서 떼어놓을 수 없다고 마음먹어 보는 건 어떨까요? 그런 식으로 해보세요. 그러면 당신의 오른손을 허벅지에서 떼어내려는 그 어떤 시도에도 정말로 저항할 수 있게 됩니다."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_UTILIZATION_TRUISM", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Utilizing resistance during hypnotic induction through physiological truisms", "tone_constraint": "Permissive, non-confrontational, and observational", "structural_constraint": "Begins with a permissive suggestion, transitions into a universal truism, and concludes with a dissociative frame separating conscious will from bodily function."}, "instruction": "내담자가 눈을 감지 않으려고 저항하는 상황에서, 그 저항을 억누르기보다 오히려 장려하면서 결국 자연스러운 생리적 현상(눈빡임)에 굴복하게 만드는 최면 유도 발화를 작성하십시오. 이때 '의식적인 노력'과 '신체적 욕구'를 분리하는 해리 기법을 포함해야 합니다.", "reasoning_trace": "첫째, 내담자의 저항(눈을 뜨고 있겠다는 의지)을 즉각적으로 수용하여 심리적 반발을 제거합니다('Perhaps you would like to keep your eyes open'). 둘째, '눈은 결국 깜빡이게 마련'이라는 거부할 수 없는 진실(Truism)을 제시하여 내담자의 무의식이 '예스 세트(Yes-Set)'를 형성하게 합니다. 셋째, 내담자에게 눈을 뜨고 있으려고 최대한 노력하라고 지시함으로써, 저항을 치료자의 지시 안으로 포섭(Utilization)합니다. 넷째, '당신의 욕구'와 '눈의 욕구'를 분리(Dissociation)하여, 신체적 반응이 의식적 통제를 벗어난 무의식적 과정임을 암시합니다. 마지막으로 '조만간(sooner or later)'이라는 시간적 모호성을 활용하여 내담자가 스스로 통제권을 내려놓을 수 있는 여지를 제공합니다.", "output": "Perhaps you would like to keep your eyes open, and I would like to have you do so as long as you can. Naturally, we all blink our eyes sooner or later. We have done that all our lives, and regardless of how hard we try to keep from blinking our eyes we are really going to blink sooner or later. But you can do your level best to hold your eyes wide open, and that may be your desire, but your eyes have a desire, too, and your eyes are going to blink."}

{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_TRUISM_PACING_LEADING", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Inducing trance through the utilization of physical fatigue", "tone_constraint": "Gentle, rhythmic, and progressively suggestive", "structural_constraint": "Use of tag questions, depersonalization (one), and logical chaining of fatigue to relaxation"}, "instruction": "내담자가 겪고 있는 신체적 긴장과 그로 인한 피로감을 활용하여, 자연스럽게 수면과 이완의 상태로 이끄는 밀턴 에릭슨 식의 유도 문구를 작성하십시오. 보편적 진리(Truism)를 활용해 거부감을 줄이고, '피로-수면-이완'의 논리적 고리를 형성해야 합니다.", "reasoning_trace": "첫째, 내담자가 현재 느끼고 있을 피로감을 '그렇지 않나요?'라는 부가 의문문(Tag question)을 통해 확인하며 라포를 형성하고 긍정적 반응 세트(Yes-set)를 유도함. 둘째, '나도 피곤함을 느낀다'는 자기 개방을 통해 피로를 지극히 정상적이고 공유된 경험으로 격상시킴. 셋째, '사람(one)'이라는 일반 지칭을 사용하여 피로가 수면으로 이어지는 과정을 보편적인 심리 법칙처럼 제시함(Depersonalization). 넷째, '점점 더', '거부할 수 없는'과 같은 강화 형용사를 사용하여 피로의 강도를 무의식적으로 증폭시킴. 다섯째, 마지막 단계에서 '당신(you)'으로 주체를 전환하여 보편적 법칙을 내담자의 개인적 경험으로 고착시키고, 피로에 대한 생각이 곧 실제 이완으로 이어지도록 인과관계를 설정함.", "output": "And that tends to make one feel rather tired, doesn’t it? And you do think about sleep when you get tired; and you do think about relaxation when you get tired; and as you think about it you relax more and more."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_TRUISM_UTILIZATION", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Resistance to hypnosis induction", "tone_constraint": "Calm, authoritative, reassuring", "structural_constraint": "Short declarative sentence using a truism and an oxymoronic intensifier"}, "instruction": "Address a resistant student volunteer by providing a sense of security that bypasses their conscious defenses using a truism and linguistic ambiguity.", "reasoning_trace": "The subject is exhibiting resistance, likely due to a fear of losing control or uncertainty about the situation. To bypass this conscious barrier, I begin with the word 'obvious,' which frames the following statement as an undeniable truth, discouraging the subject from questioning it. I then employ the phrase 'awfully safe.' The use of 'awfully'—a word typically associated with negative intensity—paired with 'safe' creates a minor semantic conflict or 'transderivational search.' This brief moment of cognitive processing allows the suggestion of safety to penetrate the unconscious mind more effectively. {"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_PACING_TRUISM", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Avoiding resistance through pacing current reality", "tone_constraint": "Calm, non-imposing, and matter-of-fact", "structural_constraint": "Use of temporal conjunctions (As...) to link undeniable physical facts"}, "instruction": "내담자의 저항을 최소화하면서 최면적 유도 상태로 이끌기 위해, 현재 내담자가 경험하고 있는 명백한 물리적 사실들을 활용하여 문장을 구성하십시오.", "reasoning_trace": "내담자의 비판적 사고를 우회하기 위해 '트루이즘(Truism)' 기법을 사용한다. 내담자가 부정할 수 없는 물리적 사실인 '의자에 앉아 있음'과 '목소리를 듣고 있음'을 'As(~함에 따라/하면서)'라는 접속사로 연결하여 논리적 타당성을 확보한다. 이는 내담자의 잠재의식 속에 'Yes-Set(긍정적 수용 상태)'을 형성하게 하며, 이후에 이어질 최면적 암시를 거부감 없이 받아들일 수 있는 심리적 교량 역할을 한다. 강요가 아닌 단순한 관찰의 진술을 통해 내담자의 통제권을 존중하는 듯한 인상을 주면서도, 실제로는 치료자의 인도에 따르도록 유도하는 고도의 전략적 언어 패턴이다.", "output": "As you sit in that chair, and as you continue to listen to me"}

{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_UTILIZATION_RESISTANCE", "context_analysis": {"topic": "최면 유도 중 발생하는 저항의 분석 및 활용", "tone_constraint": "차분하고 객관적이며, 내담자의 상태를 있는 그대로 수용하는 어조", "structural_constraint": "현재의 감각이나 상태를 즉각적으로 묘사하는 직접적 서술문 구조"}, "instruction": "내담자가 최면 상태로 진입하는 것에 대해 강한 심리적 저항을 보일 때, 이를 치료적 도구로 전환하기 위해 에릭슨이 사용할 법한 관찰적 발화를 작성하십시오.", "reasoning_trace": "에릭슨의 관점에서 저항은 치료를 방해하는 요소가 아니라 내담자가 현재 보여주는 가장 강력한 반응이자 에너지이다. 이 문장에서 에릭슨은 'Now'라는 부사를 사용하여 내담자의 주의를 현재의 주관적 경험에 강력하게 고정시킨다. 'You feel very resistant'라고 저항을 명시적으로 선언하는 것은 내담자의 내면 상태를 정확히 미러링(Mirroring)하는 것으로, 내담자는 자신의 저항이 인정받았다고 느낌과 동시에 치료자의 말이 사실임을 무의식적으로 긍정하게 된다(Yes-set 형성). 이는 저항이라는 부정적 에너지를 최면 유도 과정의 정당한 일부로 편입시켜, 결과적으로 저항의 날카로움을 무디게 만들고 다음 단계의 암시를 받아들일 준비를 시키는 고도의 전략적 수용 기법이다.", "output": "Now you feel very resistant about going into a trance."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_UTILIZATION_REFRAME", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Shifting from resistance to therapeutic alliance", "tone_constraint": "Calm, permissive, non-confrontational, and logically persuasive", "structural_constraint": "Concession of the struggle (Pacing) followed by a pivot to the patient's primary goal (Leading)"}, "instruction": "내담자가 최면 유도에 대해 강한 저항을 보일 때, 그 저항과 싸우는 대신 저항의 무의미함을 일깨우고 치료의 본질적인 목적으로 주의를 전환시키는 에릭슨적 대화 기법을 사용하십시오.", "reasoning_trace": "먼저 내담자의 저항(최면 유도 여부에 대한 논쟁)을 '하루 종일이라도 싸울 수 있는 것'으로 인정함으로써 심리적 반발(Reactance)을 제거합니다. 'I suppose'와 같은 완곡한 표현을 사용하여 강요하지 않는 분위기를 조성한 뒤, 'but'이라는 전환어를 통해 내담자가 병원을 방문한 근본적인 동기인 '도움을 받고 싶어 함'이라는 자명한 사실(Truism)로 초점을 옮깁니다. 마지막으로 치료자 자신의 의도를 '문제를 이해하고 싶다'는 협력적 태도로 정의함으로써, 힘겨루기의 장을 치료적 동맹의 장으로 재구성합니다.", "output": "Well, I suppose we could really fight it out all day on this matter of whether or not I can put you in a trance, but I really don’t believe you came here for that purpose. I think you came here to be helped in some way, and I would like to understand your problem."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_ANALOGICAL_MARKING", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Shifting focus from the problem to the process of understanding", "tone_constraint": "Subtle, deliberate, and hypnotic", "structural_constraint": "A simple declarative sentence with strategic vocal emphasis on the verb"}, "instruction": "내담자가 문제 자체에 집착하기보다 '이해'라는 치유적 과정에 주의를 기울이도록 유도하는 에릭슨의 강조 기법을 적용하여 발화하십시오.", "reasoning_trace": "에릭슨은 언어적 메시지 내에서 특정 단어를 강조(Analogical Marking)함으로써 내담자의 무의식이 주의를 기울여야 할 방향을 재설정합니다. 이 문장에서 'understand'를 강조하는 이유는, 내담자가 자신의 'problem'에 매몰되어 있는 상태에서 벗어나 치료자와 함께 '이해'라는 능동적이고 해결 지향적인 인지 과정으로 이동하게 만들기 위함입니다. 'I would like to'는 표면적으로는 치료자의 의지 표현이지만, 강조된 'understand'는 내담자에게 '이해될 수 있다'는 암시적 안도감과 변화의 가능성을 동시에 전달합니다.", "output": "I would like to *understand* your problem."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_REFRAMING_CONTRAST", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Clarifying therapeutic intent and reducing resistance", "tone_constraint": "Calm, non-confrontational, professional yet empathetic", "structural_constraint": "Contrastive statement using 'not X, but Y' structure"}, "instruction": "내담자의 저항을 줄이기 위해, 갈등 상황을 협력적 이해의 틀로 재구성하는 밀턴 에릭슨의 대화 기법을 사용하여 발화하십시오.", "reasoning_trace": "먼저 내담자가 느끼고 있을 수 있는 잠재적 갈등이나 저항(fighting)을 명시적으로 언급하여 이를 표면화하고 거부합니다. 이는 내담자의 방어 기제를 완화하는 효과가 있습니다. 그 다음, '대신에(instead)'라는 전환어를 사용하여 치료의 본질적인 목적이자 긍정적인 가치인 '이해(understanding)'로 초점을 옮깁니다. {"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_UTILIZATION_RESISTANCE", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Dealing with patient resistance through concession", "tone_constraint": "Permissive, non-confrontational, and pragmatic", "structural_constraint": "Acknowledge the possibility of conflict followed by a pivot to a constructive goal"}, "instruction": "내담자의 저항을 정면으로 돌파하려 하기보다, 그 저항이 일어날 수 있음을 인정(concession)함으로써 힘의 대결을 무력화하고, 치료적 목적으로 주의를 전환하는 밀턴 에릭슨의 대화 방식을 구현하십시오.", "reasoning_trace": "첫째, 내담자가 가진 저항의 에너지를 인정합니다. {"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_UTILIZATION_EXPERIENCE", "context_analysis": {"topic": "The source of clinical expertise and psychiatric knowledge.", "tone_constraint": "Experiential, humble yet profoundly authoritative, grounded in sensory observation.", "structural_constraint": "Negative-Positive contrastive structure using 'not A, but B' to shift focus from theory to practice."}, "instruction": "Explain your educational background in psychiatry by contrasting traditional academic learning with the wisdom gained from direct patient interaction, emphasizing that the patient is the true teacher.", "reasoning_trace": "To answer the question about the origin of my knowledge, I must first deconstruct the listener's expectation that expertise comes from formal institutions. I start by using negation ('not in the lecture room', 'not by listening to some professor') to clear away the rigid, theoretical frameworks that often hinder true clinical understanding. This creates a vacuum that I fill with the 'Utilization' principle—the idea that the solution and the knowledge reside within the patient. By repeating the phrase 'by listening to... by observing...', I am modeling the very behavior I want the listener to adopt: sensory-based awareness. This response serves a dual purpose: it establishes my credibility as someone who truly 'sees' the person in front of me, and it subtly instructs the patient that their own behavior and words are the most important data in the room.", "output": "I learned most of my psychiatry—not in the lecture room, not by listening to some professor—but by listening to my patients, by observing my patients."}

{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_INFLECTION", "context_analysis": {"topic": "The role of vocal nuance and inflection in hypnotic commands.", "tone_constraint": "Educational, precise, and subtly authoritative.", "structural_constraint": "A short, direct command used as a demonstration of linguistic precision."}, "instruction": "Explain the significance of vocal inflection in hypnotic suggestions, specifically using the phrase 'Wake up, all over' to illustrate how a patient might respond to the literal sound rather than the intended meaning.", "reasoning_trace": "Erickson focuses on the 'literalness' of the unconscious mind. He observes that if the therapist's voice drops in pitch or volume on the word 'all', or if there is a micro-pause after 'Wake up', the unconscious may interpret 'all over' as a separate, less urgent instruction or even ignore it. The reasoning here is to demonstrate that communication is not just verbal content but a tonal map. By isolating the phrase 'Wake up, all over', Erickson shows that the patient's failure to wake up completely was a successful response to the inflection provided, proving the therapist's responsibility for the outcome. He emphasizes that the unconscious mind is a keen observer of the music of speech, not just the lyrics.", "output": "Wake up, all over."}

{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_TRUISM_AND_NON_CHALLENGE", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Deepening hypnosis through goal-oriented acceptance and normalization of trance depth.", "tone_constraint": "Permissive, reassuring, and non-judgmental yet subtly authoritative.", "structural_constraint": "Use of 'either/or' truisms, strategic admission of ignorance ('neither you nor I know'), and repetitive goal-focused suggestions."}, "instruction": "내담자가 최면의 깊이에 대해 가질 수 있는 불안이나 저항을 제거하고, 최면 상태의 깊이와 관계없이 치료적 성과를 얻을 수 있다는 믿음을 심어주어 자연스럽게 필요한 수준의 트랜스로 유도하는 에릭슨적 발화를 작성하십시오.", "reasoning_trace": "먼저, 최면의 깊이가 사람마다 다를 수 있다는 일반적인 사실(Truism)을 제시하여 내담자가 어떤 상태에 있든 그것이 '정상'임을 인지시킵니다. 'Requisite(필요한)'라는 단어를 반복하여 무의식이 스스로 적절한 깊이를 찾아가도록 유도하는 것이 핵심입니다.", "output": "Now, some patients need to go very deeply into hypnosis, and some patients can accomplish everything they need to accomplish while they are in a very light stage of hypnosis. Neither you nor I know what degree of hypnosis is requisite for you, but I think you are willing to develop that degree of hypnosis that is requisite for you to give your full attention to the therapeutic accomplishments that you need. I think that you are willing to develop that degree of hypnosis that is requisite for you to accomplish the therapeutic achievements that you desires."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_INDIRECT_SUGGESTION_DISSOCIATION", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Expanding hypnotic phenomena and deepening trance through dissociation", "tone_constraint": "Casual, permissive, non-authoritarian, and dismissive of importance to bypass resistance", "structural_constraint": "Use of linguistic softeners (incidentally, not important), permissive verbs (can, might), and parallel processing clauses (while... while...)"}, "instruction": "내담자가 현재 경험 중인 강직증(catalepsy)에 더해, 신체적 안락함과 이완, 감각 마비, 그리고 특정 신체 부위에 대한 망각을 유도하십시오. '중요하지 않다'는 식의 가벼운 표현을 사용하여 저항을 최소화하고, 의식적인 분석 활동과 무의식적 현상을 병렬적으로 배치하여 깊은 트랜스 상태를 유도하는 에릭슨의 화법을 구사하십시오.", "reasoning_trace": "에릭슨은 내담자의 저항을 줄이기 위해 '부수적으로(incidentally)' 혹은 '중요하지 않다(not really important)'는 표현을 사용하여 제안의 무게감을 의도적으로 낮춥니다. 이는 의식의 비판적 검열을 우회하는 전형적인 기법입니다. 또한, '즐길 수 있다(can enjoy)'는 허용적 언어를 통해 내담자에게 통제권을 부여하는 동시에, 강직증이라는 기존 상태에 안락함, 이완, 마비, 망각이라는 새로운 현상을 층층이 쌓아 올립니다. 마지막으로 '관찰하고, 재검토하고, 생각하고, 분석하는' 의식적 활동을 신체적 현상과 '동시에(~하는 동안)' 수행하도록 지시함으로써, 의식은 분석에 몰두하게 하고 무의식은 최면 현상을 심화하도록 분리(dissociation)를 유도합니다. 이러한 다중 작업 부여는 의식을 과부하시켜 트랜스를 심화시키는 효과가 있습니다.", "output": "You know, in addition to catalepsy, you might, incidentally—and it is not really important—enjoy other phenomena of hypnosis. You can enjoy the catalepsy, but you can also enjoy a feeling of physical comfort, a feeling of relaxation, a feeling of numbness. You can enjoy forgetting that you have feet while you are enjoying being aware of the catalepsy, while you are viewing and reviewing and thinking and analyzing your various thoughts and feelings."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_PERMISSIVE_SUGGESTION", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Hypnotic induction and patient comfort", "tone_constraint": "Gentle, permissive, and non-authoritarian", "structural_constraint": "Use of indirect phrasing and progressive deepening language"}, "instruction": "Create a hypnotic suggestion that facilitates a trance state by focusing on the patient's comfort, using indirect phrasing to minimize psychological resistance.", "reasoning_trace": "To achieve the goal of inducing a trance while maintaining patient comfort, I must avoid direct commands which might trigger the conscious mind's resistance. Instead, I will use the phrase 'I would like to have you...', which frames the suggestion as a personal preference or a collaborative invitation rather than a rigid instruction. I will employ the repetition of 'deeper and deeper' to create a rhythmic, progressive sensation of sinking into a state. By using the conjunction 'as', I link the inevitable process of 'going into hypnosis' with the positive sensation of 'comfort'. This creates a presupposition that as one happens, the other must also occur, guiding the patient's focus toward their internal experience of ease.", "output": "I would like to have you enjoy a deeper and deeper feeling of comfort as you go into hypnosis."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_PERMISSIVE_LEARNING_FRAME", "context_analysis": {"topic": "The individual pace of learning hypnosis and psychological change", "tone_constraint": "Patient, non-judgmental, authoritative yet permissive, and strategically curious.", "structural_constraint": "Uses a descending numerical sequence to imply rapid progress, followed by a 'not-knowing' stance and a permissive frame for curiosity."}, "instruction": "Explain to a patient that learning hypnosis is a personal process with varying speeds. Use numerical comparisons to lower performance anxiety while subtly suggesting the possibility of rapid learning, and grant the patient full permission to explore their own curiosity without pressure.", "reasoning_trace": "1. Identify and Neutralize Performance Anxiety: I start with a rhetorical question ('do you expect to accomplish... 100 times the very first time?') to immediately lower the patient's self-imposed pressure to perform perfectly at the start. 2. Progressive Suggestion of Efficiency: By using a descending sequence (100 to 50, then 20, then 5), I create a mental 'slide' that shifts the focus from 'if' they can learn to 'how fast' they might learn, subtly suggesting they could be more efficient than they realize. 3. The 'Not-Knowing' Stance: By stating 'I really don’t know how fast you can learn,' I bypass the patient's conscious resistance. If I don't set a limit, their unconscious is free to set its own, often faster, pace. 4. Utilization of Autonomy: I use permissive language ('perfectly willing to have you learn as rapidly as you wish') to return the sense of control to the patient, which is crucial for deep trance work. 5. Strategic Distraction: I encourage learning 'unimportant things' out of 'curiosity.' This occupies the conscious mind with trivialities, allowing the unconscious to process the core therapeutic work without interference. 6. Reassurance of Safety: I conclude by validating that this extra exploration is safe and won't hinder their progress, reinforcing a secure environment for change.", "output": "Now really, do you expect to accomplish what somebody else accomplishes who has worked with hypnosis 100 times the very first time I work with you? Maybe you, can accomplish in 50 times what somebody else can accomplish in 100 times; maybe you can accomplish in 20 times what somebody else would require 100 efforts at; maybe you can accomplish in 5 attempts what somebody else would require 100 attempts at. In fact, I really don’t know how fast you can learn. I am perfectly willing to have you learn as rapidly as you wish, as rapidly as you can; and I am also perfectly willing to have you learn a lot of things that aren’t really important in this situation but which you might like to learn just because of your own curiosity. I am very certain that learning extra things won’t interfere with what we are doing."}

This builds a 'yes-set' and validates the concept of parallel processing. Next, I will introduce a more complex example—knitting—to illustrate how a highly intricate task can be performed without conscious attention. By contrasting the difficulty the patient would face with the ease of an experienced knitter, I highlight the power of the unconscious mind's automaticity. Finally, I will use a linguistic bridge ('And so') to transition from these mundane examples to the therapeutic context, suggesting that hypnotic phenomena can occur just as incidentally and automatically as these everyday habits, thereby reducing the patient's need to 'try' or consciously monitor the process.", "output": "Actually, you know, you can eat and watch a television program simultaneously, and your awareness of the television program won’t be diminished by your eating. You can smoke without it being a disturbance to your viewing of the program. There are a great variety of things you can do. Your wife may knit while watching a television program; she can knit one, drop one, purl two and not miss a line of the program. Yet, she is executing a most complicated movement that would take every bit of your attention. But she does it so automatically and so effortlessly. And so, while we are working on this particular goal, you can achieve other hypnotic phenomena, incidentally and automatically."}

{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_CONFUSION_AGE_REGRESSION", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Age regression via Confusion Technique and Truism", "tone_constraint": "Casual, assertive, and matter-of-fact", "structural_constraint": "Direct suggestion embedded in a simple declarative sentence with a reinforcing tautological tag"}, "instruction": "연령 퇴행(Age Regression)을 유도하기 위해, 현재의 시점과 과거의 특정 시점을 혼동시키는 기법을 사용하여 내담자가 자연스럽게 1940년 6월의 어느 날로 이동하도록 유도하는 문장을 작성하세요.", "reasoning_trace": "에릭슨은 내담자의 비판적 사고를 우회하기 위해 '혼란 기법(Confusion Technique)'과 '자명한 이치(Truism)'를 결합합니다. 먼저 'You know'라는 일상적인 도입부로 내담자의 주의를 편안하게 고정시킨 뒤, '날씨가 좋다'는 주관적 사실과 '1940년 6월'이라는 구체적인 과거 시점을 하나의 문장으로 묶습니다. 내담자의 의식이 현재와 과거 사이의 시공간적 괴리를 처리하느라 혼란을 겪는 찰나, 'and it really is'라는 강력한 긍정의 확언을 덧붙여 논리적 검증 과정을 생략하고 해당 시점을 현실로 수용하게 만듭니다. 이는 의식이 시간적 모순을 해결하려 애쓰는 동안 무의식이 즉각적으로 과거의 기억과 감각으로 퇴행하도록 설계된 고도의 언어적 장치입니다.", "output": "You know, this is really a nice day in June, 1940, and it really is."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_UTILIZATION_DISSOCIATION", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Subconscious memory organization during the Confusion Technique", "tone_constraint": "Calm, process-oriented, permissive yet structured", "structural_constraint": "Iterative phrasing, progressive comparative structures (more and more / less and less), and focus on internal processing."}, "instruction": "Explain the process of facilitating subconscious memory organization during a trance state, emphasizing the shift of attention from the present to past learnings.", "reasoning_trace": "To explain this process in a characteristically Ericksonian way, I must first establish a sense of rhythmic progression. I start with repetitive temporal markers ('At each level, at each date') to pace the patient's internal experience. I use permissive language ('letting them recall') to minimize resistance, while framing memories as 'learnings' to imply a therapeutic purpose. The core of the technique involves a linguistic 'see-saw' effect: I use the 'more and more' structure to intensify the internal focus on the past, which naturally leads to the 'less and less' structure to diminish awareness of the present environment. This creates a functional dissociation that allows the subconscious mind to organize information without interference from the conscious 'today'.", "output": "At each level, at each date, I’m letting them recall certain learnings, certain memories; I am getting them to think more and more about the past events and the past learnings in their lives. {"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_DISSOCIATIVE_REGRESSION", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Age regression via visual hallucination on a movie screen", "tone_constraint": "Calm, observant, and subtly inquisitive", "structural_constraint": "Third-person dissociation combined with open-ended questioning"}, "instruction": "내담자가 영화 스크린을 환각하고 있는 상태에서, 어린 시절로 퇴행할 수 있도록 유도하는 밀턴 에릭슨 스타일의 발화를 작성하세요. 내담자가 자신을 객관화하여 관찰할 수 있도록 3인칭 시점을 사용하고, 무의식이 스스로 다음 행동을 결정하게끔 유도해야 합니다.", "reasoning_trace": "1. 분리(Dissociation) 기법 적용: 내담자가 과거의 자신을 직접적으로 대면할 때 느낄 수 있는 저항이나 감정적 과부하를 줄이기 위해, '당신' 대신 '한 어린 소녀'라는 3인칭 표현을 사용합니다. 이는 영화 스크린이라는 메타포를 강화합니다. 2. 공간적 고정(Spatial Anchoring): '저쪽 방향에서'와 같은 구체적인 지시어를 통해 내담자의 내적 환각 공간을 더욱 견고하게 만듭니다. 3. 페이싱(Pacing): '행복하게 걷고 있다'는 묘사를 통해 긍정적인 정서적 상태를 설정하고 현재의 환각 상태를 따라갑니다. 4. 무의식적 탐색 유도(Leading): '그녀가 다음에 무엇을 할까요?'라는 개방형 질문을 던짐으로써, 의식적인 논리가 개입하기 전에 무의식이 스스로 다음 기억이나 행동을 인출(Retrieval)하도록 유도합니다. 이는 자연스러운 연령 퇴행을 촉진하는 핵심 장치입니다.", "output": "And now a little girl appears from that direction over there, and she is walking happily along. What is she doing now? I don’t see her clearly. I couldn’t see that movement she just made... What was it? ... What is she picking up now?"}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_UTILIZATION_HALLUCINATION", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Accessing subconscious content through hallucination projection", "tone_constraint": "Curious, collaborative, and subtly authoritative", "structural_constraint": "Progression from shared observation to strategic ignorance, then to direct inquiry"}, "instruction": "내담자가 경험하고 있는 환각(어린 소녀)의 내적 대화를 탐색하도록 유도하십시오. 치료자 자신은 그 소리를 들을 수 없음을 명시하여 내담자가 유일한 정보 제공자로서의 역할을 수행하게 하고, 주의를 집중시켜 무의식적 메시지를 인출해내십시오.", "reasoning_trace": "먼저 'You know'라는 표현으로 내담자와의 공감대를 형성하며 환각의 존재를 기정사실화합니다. 치료자가 '나는 들리지 않는다'고 말하는 것은 전략적 무지(Strategic Ignorance) 기법으로, 내담자의 자아를 강화하는 동시에 내담자가 환각에 더 깊이 몰입하도록 유도합니다. {"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_INDIRECT_SENSORY_GROUNDING", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Hallucination exploration and sensory integration", "tone_constraint": "Gentle, inquisitive, permissive, and guiding", "structural_constraint": "A sequence of open-ended and sensory-specific questions"}, "instruction": "내담자가 환각 속의 인물에 대해 감정과 신체적 감각을 묘사하도록 유도하되, 직접적인 명령 대신 추측과 관찰을 제안하는 에릭슨 스타일의 질문을 작성하세요.", "reasoning_trace": "1. 목표 설정: 내담자가 생성한 환각(어린 소녀)을 더 구체화하고, 내담자의 무의식이 그 환각의 감각적 세부 사항에 깊이 몰입하게 함. 2. 투사적 질문(Supposition): '어떻게 느낀다고 생각하나요?(suppose)'라는 표현을 사용하여 내담자가 자신의 내면적 감각을 소녀에게 투사하도록 유도함. 이는 의식의 저항을 우회하는 간접적 방식임. 3. 감각적 세부화(Sensory Specifics): 발이 땅에 닿는 느낌, 그네의 움직임 등 구체적인 운동 감각(Kinesthetic)을 언급하여 환각의 생생함(Vividness)을 극대화하고 트랜스를 심화함. 4. 주의 집중 유도(Focus of Attention): '눈치챌 수 있나요?(Can you notice)'라는 질문을 통해 내담자의 주의를 환각의 외형에서 그 존재의 내부적 경험으로 자연스럽게 이동시킴. 5. 언어적 허용성: 강요하지 않고 내담자가 스스로 답을 찾아내도록 질문의 형식을 빌려 무의식적 탐색을 허용함.", "output": "And how do you suppose she feels? Can you notice how she is feeling? Does she feel the way her feet are placed on the ground? Does she feel the swing moving?"}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_DISSOCIATION_REGRESSION", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Age regression and dual consciousness", "tone_constraint": "Calm, permissive, and repetitive", "structural_constraint": "Spatial dissociation using 'here' and 'there' to separate the observer from the experiencer"}, "instruction": "내담자가 과거의 특정 경험(예: 그네 타기)에 몰입하면서도 동시에 현재의 치료자와 소통할 수 있는 이중적 의식(Dissociation) 상태를 유도하는 최면적 암시를 작성하십시오.", "reasoning_trace": "내담자의 의식을 '경험하는 자아'와 '관찰하는 자아'로 분리하는 것이 핵심입니다. 먼저 '저 위에서 그네를 타는 동안(as you swing up there)'이라는 표현을 통해 내담자의 신체적 감각을 과거의 시공간으로 보냅니다. 동시에 '내 옆에 있는 목소리(a voice here beside me)'라는 표현을 사용하여, 내담자의 일부분은 여전히 치료자와 함께 있으며 대화가 가능하다는 것을 암시합니다. {"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_AGE_REGRESSION_SPATIAL_UTILIZATION", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Hypnotic age regression and spatial navigation demonstration", "tone_constraint": "Authoritative, rhythmic, and process-oriented", "structural_constraint": "Temporal anchoring followed by repetitive, sequential motor commands"}, "instruction": "Demonstrate a method to validate age regression by instructing a subject to navigate a specific past environment through sequential physical movements.", "reasoning_trace": "First, I establish a firm temporal anchor by specifying the year 1934, which immediately shifts the subject's orientation from the present to the past. Second, I employ 'spatial utilization' by directing her to perform specific physical actions—walking and turning—within that hallucinated past. This requires the unconscious mind to construct a detailed, three-dimensional mental map of a location she knew at that time. Third, I use repetitive phrasing ('walk down... turn... walk down') to create a rhythmic cadence that deepens the trance and reinforces the reality of the experience. By leaving the specific street names as 'such-and-such', I allow the subject's own unconscious memory to fill in the authentic details, making the regression more vivid and undeniable for the observer, McTear.", "output": "All right, make it the year 1934, and have her walk down such-and-such a street; then have her turn and walk down another street, and then have her turn and walk down another street."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_PACING_LEADING", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Testing trance depth and responsiveness through guided imagery", "tone_constraint": "Calm, directive, and matter-of-fact", "structural_constraint": "Present tense, sequential logic using a causal bridge (so)"}, "instruction": "최면 상태의 내담자에게 특정 공간적 이미지를 제시하고, 그 흐름에 맞춰 자연스럽게 행동을 유도하는 페이싱(Pacing)과 리딩(Leading) 기법의 발화를 작성하십시오.", "reasoning_trace": "먼저 'Now'라는 단어를 사용하여 내담자의 주의를 현재의 주관적 경험으로 고정시킵니다. 그 다음 'coming to the intersection'이라는 구체적인 상황을 제시하여 내담자가 무의식 속에서 해당 이미지를 구성하도록 유도(Pacing)합니다. {"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_DISSOCIATED_PROJECTION", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Using age regression and dissociation to address a psychosomatic symptom (vomiting).", "tone_constraint": "Speculative, detached, and inquisitive, avoiding direct confrontation with the patient's ego.", "structural_constraint": "Use of third-person reference ('that person') and temporal shifting (future pacing) to bypass conscious resistance."}, "instruction": "내담자가 자신의 증상을 타자화하여 객관적으로 관찰하고, 무의식적인 해결책을 스스로 제시할 수 있도록 해리(Dissociation)와 미래 투사(Future Projection) 기법을 사용하여 대화하십시오.", "reasoning_trace": "먼저, 내담자를 '저기 서 있는 사람(that person standing there)'으로 지칭함으로써 자아와 증상 사이에 심리적 거리를 두는 '해리' 상태를 유도합니다. 이는 내담자가 자신의 문제를 자신의 것으로 느끼지 않고 제3자의 관점에서 관찰하게 하여 방어 기제를 약화시킵니다. 그 다음, '1년, 2년, 3년 후'라는 시간적 왜곡(Time Distortion)을 통해 미래의 시점으로 이동시킵니다. 여기서 구토 반응을 미래에 일어날 수 있는 가상의 사건으로 설정함으로써, 현재의 고통스러운 증상을 '처리해야 할 과제'로 객관화합니다. 마지막으로, 내담자에게 그 사람이 어떻게 행동해야 할지 '추측(speculate)'하고 '조언'하도록 요청함으로써, 내담자 내부의 자원과 무의식적 지혜가 스스로 해결책을 찾아내도록 유도하는 전략을 취했습니다.", "output": "You know, if you look at that person standing there, you can speculate about what the future will be. {"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_UTILIZATION_AND_REFRAMING", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Addressing hypnosis skepticism and nail-biting habit", "tone_constraint": "Provocative, ironic, authoritative, and paradoxically empathetic", "structural_constraint": "Begins with a truism, bypasses resistance via a rhetorical question, and concludes with a psychological reframing of the habit as a failed endeavor."}, "instruction": "A 26-year-old male client who bites his fingernails is skeptical about being hypnotized. Establish a 'Yes-Set' by stating an undeniable truth ('I see that you are here') to ground the client in the present moment. 2. Pivot the focus from the therapy process to the physical reality of his problem ('stubby fingernails'), using slightly derogatory language to create a mild aversion. 4. Use temporal distortion by emphasizing the 22-year duration (from age 4 to 26), making the habit feel like an exhausting, lifelong burden. 5. Reframe the habit not as a moral failing or a nervous tic, but as a 'failed investment.' By describing the reward as a 'teensy, teensy little piece,' I highlight the absurdity and lack of satisfaction in the act. 6. Twenty-two years of frustration!"}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_SYMPTOM_PRESCRIPTION", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Nail-biting habit modification", "tone_constraint": "Authoritative, logical, yet paradoxically permissive", "structural_constraint": "Direct suggestion using fractionation and utilization of the symptom"}, "instruction": "손톱을 깨무는 습관이 있는 내담자에게, 습관을 억지로 멈추게 하는 대신 오히려 그 습관을 활용하여 점진적으로 변화를 이끌어낼 수 있는 에릭슨식 역설적 개입을 제안하십시오.", "reasoning_trace": "첫째, 내담자의 저항을 줄이기 위해 습관 전체를 금지하지 않고 '10개의 손가락'이라는 물리적 사실을 활용하여 선택지를 좁힙니다(Fractionation). 둘째, '하나 정도는 내어줄 수 있다'는 논리를 통해 변화를 위한 작은 양보를 이끌어냅니다. 셋째, 손톱을 기르는 행위의 목적을 '더 잘 깨물기 위해서'로 재정의(Reframing)하여, 내담자의 기존 욕구를 부정하지 않으면서도 실제로는 손톱이 자라나는 성공 경험을 유도합니다. 넷째, '가치 있는 씹을 거리'라는 표현을 통해 지연된 보상을 암시하며, 손톱이 자라는 동안 내담자가 자신의 통제력을 무의식적으로 연습하게 만듭니다.", "output": "Now what I am going to suggest to you is this: You have ten fingers. {"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_AUTHORITY_REFRAMING", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Establishing therapeutic alliance and authority with a child patient regarding thumb-sucking.", "tone_constraint": "Authoritative, protective, and professionally distancing from parental interference.", "structural_constraint": "Logical syllogism and role-redefinition within a professional hierarchy."}, "instruction": "엄지손가락을 빠는 아이에게 부모의 간섭을 배제하고 의사와 환자 사이의 독점적이고 비밀스러운 유대감을 형성하여 치료적 권위를 확립하는 말을 하시오.", "reasoning_trace": "내담자인 아이에게 '환자'라는 특별한 지위를 부여하여 부모의 통제로부터 보호받는 느낌을 제공합니다. 아버지는 외부인으로, 간호사인 어머니는 의사의 조력자로 재정의함으로써 부모의 잔소리를 차단하고 오직 치료사와의 관계에만 집중하게 유도합니다. 이는 아이의 저항을 줄이고 치료적 암시가 효과적으로 작동할 수 있는 심리적 공간을 확보하기 위한 고도의 전략적 접근입니다.", "output": "Jimmy, your father and mother have asked me to take care of you because you suck your thumb. You are just my patient."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_UTILIZATION_TRUISM", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Addressing a child's thumb-sucking habit through ownership and autonomy.", "tone_constraint": "Authoritative yet permissive, matter-of-fact, and non-judgmental.", "structural_constraint": "Use of repetitive truisms, possessive pronouns to emphasize ownership, and a paradoxical permission-giving frame."}, "instruction": "Create a therapeutic dialogue for a child named Jimmy who sucks his left thumb, using Milton Erickson's technique of utilization and establishing personal autonomy to bypass resistance.", "reasoning_trace": "First, I must establish a 'Yes-set' by stating undeniable physical facts (truisms) such as 'your thumb is your thumb.' This builds immediate rapport and subconscious agreement. Second, I will shift the psychological frame from 'a bad habit that needs to be stopped by an adult' to 'personal property owned by the child.' By emphasizing 'your thumb' and 'your teeth,' I am returning the responsibility of the body parts to Jimmy. Third, I will use paradoxical intention by giving him full permission to do whatever he wants. This eliminates the power struggle; if he is 'allowed' to do it, the act of sucking his thumb is no longer a form of rebellion or a secret comfort against authority, but a conscious choice he must now manage himself. The repetition of 'get that straight' adds a serious, contract-like tone to this empowerment.", "output": "Now, let us get one thing straight. That left thumb of yours is your thumb; that mouth of yours is your mouth; those front teeth of yours are your front teeth. I think you are entitled to do anything you want to with your thumb, with your mouth, and with your teeth. They are yours, and let us get that straight. I want you to do anything you want to do."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_UTILIZATION_INVITATION", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Family therapy and paternal involvement", "tone_constraint": "Permissive, expectant, and non-confrontational", "structural_constraint": "Direct open-ended interrogative"}, "instruction": "지미의 아버지가 현재 상황에 대해 자신의 생각이나 감정을 털어놓도록 유도하되, 강요하지 않고 자연스럽게 발언권을 넘기는 에릭슨 스타일의 질문을 하세요.", "reasoning_trace": "에릭슨은 치료 과정에서 내담자 주변 인물의 반응을 '이용(Utilization)'하는 데 탁월했습니다. 이 짧은 질문은 세 가지 심리적 층위를 가집니다. 첫째, 'Have you got anything'이라는 표현은 발언의 내용이나 양에 제한을 두지 않는 '허용적(Permissive)' 접근입니다. 둘째, 질문의 간결함은 상대방으로 하여금 그 공백을 자신의 생각으로 채우게 만드는 '심리적 압박(Minimalist Pressure)'을 유도합니다. 셋째, 아버지를 대화의 주체로 끌어들임으로써 가족 시스템 내의 역동을 관찰하고, 그가 내뱉는 첫 마디를 치료의 새로운 단서로 활용하려는 전략적 포석입니다. 이는 저항을 줄이면서도 자발적인 참여를 이끌어내는 전형적인 에릭슨적 개입입니다.", "output": "Have you got anything to say?"}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_TRUISM_REFRAME", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Habit modification (thumb sucking) through social norms", "tone_constraint": "Gentle, authoritative, instructional, and subtly provocative", "structural_constraint": "Sequential truisms followed by a moral/logical reframe of the symptom"}, "instruction": "지미(Jimmy)의 엄지손가락을 빠는 습관을 교정하기 위해, 아이가 이미 알고 있는 '순서 지키기(taking turns)'라는 사회적 규칙을 활용하여 무의식적인 변화를 유도하는 밀턴 에릭슨의 대화를 구성하십시오.", "reasoning_trace": "먼저, 내담자가 거부할 수 없는 사실들(Truisms)을 나열하여 'Yes-Set'을 형성합니다. 유치원, 초등학교, 가정에서의 식사 시간 등 지미가 이미 완벽하게 습득한 '차례 지키기'의 개념을 상기시켜 학습 상태(Learning State)를 활성화합니다. 그 다음, 이 보편적인 규칙을 지미의 문제 행동(왼쪽 엄지 빨기)에 교묘하게 대입합니다. 여기서 핵심은 습관을 '나쁜 것'으로 규정하여 저항을 일으키는 대신, 오른쪽 엄지손가락에게는 기회를 주지 않는 '불공평하고 옳지 못한 행동'으로 재프레임(Reframing)하는 것입니다. 이는 지미의 도덕심과 공정함을 자극하여, 기존의 자동적인 습관을 의식적인 선택의 영역으로 끌어올리고 결국 습관의 패턴을 깨뜨리도록 설계되었습니다.", "output": "I have a little more to say to you, Jimmy. One of the first things you learned when you went to nursery school was to take turns. You took turns with this little girl and with that little boy in doing things in nursery school. You learned to take turns in the first grade. In fact, you learned to take turns at home. When Mother serves the food she serves it first to one brother, and then it may be your turn, then it may be sister’s turn, then it is Mother’s turn. We always do things by turns. But I don’t think you are being right or fair or good in always sucking your left thumb and never giving your right thumb a turn."}
{"pattern_id": "ERICKSON_UTILIZATION_REFRAMING", "context_analysis": {"topic": "Habit modification regarding thumb sucking", "tone_constraint": "Gentle, non-judgmental, and authoritative yet playful", "structural_constraint": "Observation of current state -> Positive identity reinforcement -> Presupposition of a new desire for fairness"}, "instruction": "Jimmy라는 아이의 손가락 빨기 습관을 교정하기 위해, 아이를 비난하지 않으면서도 습관의 패턴을 변화시킬 수 있는 에릭슨 방식의 대화를 구성하세요.
